kicker: ET LEVEL I // SECONDARY FIELD & IMPEDANCE

beats.narration: Last lesson your coil planted eddy currents in the part. Now the part answers back, and everything the instrument reads starts right here.
Eddy currents are real currents, so by Lenz's law their secondary field opposes the coil's. That drops reactance, raises resistance, and the impedance point moves.

payload.heading: The secondary field loads the coil
payload.note: SECONDARY FIELD OPPOSES (LENZ) -> XL DOWN, R UP

beats.narration: Think transformer: coil is the primary, the part a one-turn shorted secondary. Its load reflects back two ways every cycle.

chips.s: Eddy current heat drains the coil
Couples coil to part across the gap

beats.narration: The coil opposes alternating current two ways, ninety degrees apart. Resistance burns energy; reactance equals two pi f L. Their vector sum is impedance.

payload.heading: R, XL, and their vector sum Z

tiles.s: Square root R squared + XL squared

beats.narration: Conductivity, lift-off, a crack: every variable bends the eddy currents, the secondary field, and the coil's impedance. Reading that change is the whole method.

payload.sub: Watch one coil's impedance — that is eddy current testing.
